summary: sTec board determined to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec.
An investment bank representing Company A contacted sTec management to request a preliminary meeting to evaluate a possible business transaction.

Balch Hill Partners filed a Schedule 13D with the SEC reporting a 6.4% stake in sTec and subsequently launched an activist campaign urging the board to reduce costs, focus products, and explore strategic alternatives, including nominating directors for election.
type_fields: {"other_detail": "Schedule 13D filing and activist campaign urging strategic alternatives", "process_kind": "public_announcement", "process_scope": "activist"}

summary: Company B's President stated intention to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B's management.
Board established a special committee to conduct a review of sTec's strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the company.
Company B's management determined it was not interested in acquiring sTec, believing it could independently develop the needed technology.
Company C expressed a potential interest in exploring an acquisition of particular assets of sTec's business.
Company D contacted sTec management to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.

sTec board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch as financial advisor and determined to confidentially approach strategic buyers most likely to have interest in a potential acquisition, while also conducting a limited confirmatory exploration of financial buyer interest.
sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
